subject: DevOps
title: Canary Releases
description: A micro-lesson that makes Canary Releases usable.

hook.text: Coal miners brought canaries underground. If the bird stopped singing, the air was toxic. Get out now.

buildup.text: A canary release sends new code to a small group of users first — say 2%. They are the canary.

discovery.text: Monitor the canary group closely. If error rates spike, roll back before the other 98% ever see the bug.

twist.text: If the canary sings — metrics look good — gradually increase to 10%, 50%, then 100% of traffic.

climax.text: Canary releases combine real user traffic with controlled risk. No staging environment can replicate that.

punchline.text: Test with real users, tiny risk. Canary finds danger first.

quiz.question: What percentage of users typically receives a canary release first?
options: A very small group, such as 2%
